title: Ingress, Gateway API 1.5.1, Routing, TLS, Delegation, and Status · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Gatewayclass Controller Capabilities
summary: Platform and application teams must share external HTTP routing without annotation-specific coupling, cross-namespace privilege, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s gatewayclass controller capabilities increment in Gateway API routing and delegation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select GatewayClass and implementation from controller identity, parameters, supported features, conformance reports, release channel, and operational ownership.
Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence. Competency probe: Select GatewayClass and implementation from controller identity, parameters, supported features, conformance reports, release channel, and operational ownership. A defensible solution applies K8s Gatewayclass Controller Capabilities, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to Gateway API routing and delegation policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-gatewayclass-controller-capabilities — Select GatewayClass and implementation from controller identity, parameters, supported features, conformance reports, release channel, and operational ownership.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-gateway-ingress-routing-tls-2
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: worked k8s gateway ingress routing tls 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-gatewayclass-controller-capabilities-telsqn
  - competency: k8s-gatewayclass-controller-capabilities
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: gatewayclass controller capabilities
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: bound claim snapshot checksum and application consistency query
    case-key: telsqn
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.
